No.

Okay.
Where do you live?
Or where are you staying?
If we're talking to an elderly person, we'll say mungalakuti.

I am staying or I live in, in Nyanja, we'll say mungalaku.

Okay.

I am going.
In Nyanja, we'll say naenda.
Okay.

segments.text: Hey everybody, welcome back to my channel.
So on today's video, I'll be translating full Nyanja sentences.
So let's go straight into the video.
Mwuli bwanj, how are you?
Bwino mwuli bwanj, fine, how are you?
Mwachokelakut, where are you coming from?
Kumaliket, the market.
So it's rare that I hear people use maliket.
You can just say kumaliket, it's okay.
Mwagulachani, what did you buy?
Ndiyo, relish.
Nagulansomba, nkuku na nyama.
I bought fish, chicken and meat.
So nsomba is fish, nkuku is chicken, nyama is meat.
So it can be any type of meat, it's still nyama.
Tizadja chani palanch, what are we having for lunch?
Tizadja nsima na nkuku.
We'll have nsima with chicken.
So I hear a lot of people saying shima.
But for me, I think the correct pronunciation is nsima.
Whether in English or Chinyanja, it's still nsima.
So thank you so much for watching.
Please let me know what you think about the video in the comments section below.

so that you receive a notification each time I post a new video.
Thank you, bye!

ihAtAVom2sI.title: Useful Nyanja phrases used when shopping
ihAtAVom2sI.prompt_used: This is a Nyanja (Chinyanja) lesson by a Zambian creator. She teaches everyday Nyanja vocabulary and phrases. Common Nyanja: moni, zikomo, ndikufuna, bwanji, mwana (child), mayi (mother), bambo (fathe...

segments.text: Welcome back to my channel. So today's video is on shopping. I'm going to be
sharing some of the useful Nyanja phrases that you can use
when shopping, like especially if you're buying
on the street or buying from the market, like you're buying vegetables, fruits
from Soweto market. For those that do not know Soweto market,
Soweto market is one of the biggest markets here in Zambia
and you can find fresh vegetables and fresh fruits from there.
How much are you selling?
Mungulitsazingati, that's a Nyanja.
Give me one kg. A Nyanja nipaseni one kg. But let's say maybe
you want to buy three mangoes, three apples,
you will say nipaseni mango itatu. So itatu is three.
Nipaseni is give me. If you just want one, you say nipaseni mango imozi,
which is one.
Give me very big tomatoes. In Nyanja, nipaseni tomatoikuluikulu.
So ikuluikulu is very big in Nyanja.
I don't want very ripe tomatoes. In Nyanja, sinifuna tomatoi opiesa.
So I don't want is sinifuna. In Nyanja, opiesa is very ripe.
And then if it's raw tomatoes, we say iwisi, meaning it's not ripe.
So iwisi.
Most of you people will agree with me to say the town Nyanja has some bimba in it,
especially when it comes to names of foods.
So please don't come for me, but you're free to correct me
in the comment section below. So this vegetable is called lumanda.
Very nice, very nice, especially with groundnuts.
Then that's okra, known as delele.
Then that's carpenter.
Then this is dry fish. It's called nsomba. So whether dry or
fresh, it's still nsomba. Then that's cassava, known as tute.
Mushrooms, known as boa. Garden eggs,
known as impua.
Groundnuts, known as nshaba.
Caterpillars, known as vinguwala.
And then those are pumpkin leaves, known as chihuahua.

I have bathed, okay?
Then the next one is kujua, kujua, so kujua is cutting, example, kajue mitengo, go and
cut the trees, or juwa cabbage, cut the cabbage.
The next one is kutamba, kutamba is watching, example, nitamba, movie, I am watching a movie,
or nitamba TV, I am watching TV.
The next one is kupianga, kupianga is sweeping, kapiangi, go and sweep, okay?
Then the next one is kumwa, kumwa is drinking, ukumwachani, what are you drinking, nikumuamanzi,
I am drinking water.
Then the next one is kusegula, kusegula is opening, segula do, open the door, niseguli
leko boto, open the bottle, kuyenza, kuyenza is driving, okay?
For example, dine nienza, I am the one driving.
That's all I had for you, please tell me what you think about the video in the comment
section below.

bell so that you receive a notification each time I post a new video.

The next one is kupianga, kupianga is sweeping, kapiangi, go and sweep, okay? Then the next one is kumwa, kumwa is drinking, ukumwachani, what are you drinking, nikumuamanzi, I am drinking water. Then the next one is kusegula, kusegula is opening, segula do, open the door, niseguli leko boto, open the bottle, kuyenza, kuyenza is driving, okay? For example, dine nienza, I am the one driving.
